TRAITS
Link Attributes when calculating skill advancement for the skii (see p. 211). The cost of Natural Aptitude is Character Point for non: combat skill; or 2 Character Points for combat skill (as deter mined by the gamemaster). character may never have more than one Natural Aptitude without the gamemaster' specific permission (Clan trueborns are notable exception to this rule). Natural Aptitude must be purchased separately for Sub skills with the exception of   Bureaucracy; Protocol and Streetwise In these cases_ the aptitude is considered to cover all regional subskills Of course separate subskills for each region must be acquired as usual, but the aptitude applies to them_ For example character with Natural Aptitude / Streetwise and the skills Streetwise / Luthien and Streetwise /Capellan Confederation would be   considered have Natural Aptitude with both subskills_
NIGHT BLINDNESS Value: The character has very poor night vision_ such that all tar get number modifiers for darkness are doubled,
NIGHT VISION Cost: character with this trait has better-than average   night vision, although he is not actually able to see in complete dark- ness. This trait reduces the target number modifiers associated with darkness by half (round down).
lost foot reduces the character' $ effective RFL by and cuts all movement rates in half. Iost leg reduces the charac ter'$ RFL half (round down); makes running; sprinting and evading movement impossible; ad cuts walking movement to one-quarter the normal speed: Note also that if the lost Mimo is replaced by tully func tional permanent  prosthetic (Type 5), the loss is effectively cured_ and it is worth no Character Points_
OWNS VEHICLE Cost: Through inheritance, hard work or sheer luck the character actually owns his BattleMech (or other vehicle) if he spends a additional points on it.
Vehicle Costs Owning vehicle can be an expensive proposition_ Without the Owns Vehicle Trait, these expenses are taken care of by the unit that owns the vehicle With this trait, the responsibility of maintenance and upkeep falls on the player character' shoul: ders Basic maintenance for any vehicle except spacecraft costs 100 C-bills per week not counting the costs of repairs Or ammunition; the upkeep of DropShips and JumpShips costs 500 Cbills per week; In addition, every vehicle needs tO be attended by trained technician to stay in working order: If the owning character actually trained in the appropriate Technician Field(s) for his vehi: cle, he can take care of this himself provided he has enough free time. Likewise another player character in his group with the right skills could offer to handle the support requirements. If no such character is available_ one will have to be kept on retainer; at an additional cost of 250 Cbills per week Of course; this is vast simplification of the process of vehicle upkeep; Detailed rules for creating and maintaining military unit can found the BattleTech   Field  Manual:
MECHWARRIOR PHENOTYPE Cost: Clan trueborn MechWarriors appear physically identical to normal" humans but this appearance misleading: Generations of selective breeding ad subtle genetic manipula: tion have increased their reflexes and improved their adaptabil: ity to the neurohelmet interface So vital t0 BattleMech control, The primary advantages MechWarriors receive are modified Attribute thresholds in the Life Path (DEX +1_ RFL +1) and mod: ified Attribute maximums as noted on the Attribute Maximums Table (p. 56). Because they have been carefully designed and bred over dozens generations pilot BattleMechs MechWarriors are considered to have the Natural Aptitude Trait (below) for all the skills in the Clan MechWarrior Field.
